name: Detection of Malicious IOC in Events
notes:
This rule triggers when an IOC is categorized as malicious in a reference set collection.

Note: The rules Malware as a Service Hash IOC in Events, Ransomware: Ryuk IOC in Events have been excluded from this rule to avoid repetition, their purpose is to have a dedicated rule response.
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Operating System
when an event matches any of the following Malware as a Service Hash IOC in Events, Ransomware: Ryuk IOC in Events
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success, System.File Created, System.File Deleted
when the event matches "SHA256 Hash" is not null or "MD5 Hash" is not null or "SHA1 Hash" is not null AQL filter query

when the event matches REFERENCESETCONTAINS('Malware Hashes SHA', "SHA256 Hash") or REFERENCESETCONTAINS('Malware Hashes MD5', "MD5 Hash") or REFERENCESETCONTAINS('Malware Hashes SHA', "SHA1 Hash") AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Detection of Malicious IOC in Events on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Operating System
AND NOT when an event matches any of the following Malware as a Service Hash IOC in Events, Ransomware: Ryuk IOC in Events
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success, System.File Created, System.File Deleted
AND when the event matches "SHA256 Hash" is not null or  "MD5 Hash" is not null or "SHA1 Hash" is not null AQL filter query

AND when the event matches REFERENCESETCONTAINS('Malware Hashes SHA', "SHA256 Hash") or REFERENCESETCONTAINS('Malware Hashes MD5', "MD5 Hash") or  REFERENCESETCONTAINS('Malware Hashes SHA', "SHA1 Hash") AQL filter query
RED_list: Event description: Detects when an IOC is categorized as malicious in a reference set collection.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Machine Identifier (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
